RESPONSE TO MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S PETITION FILED BY RESPONDENT SPARE BACKUP, INC.
RE-NOTICE OF PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION (AND APPOINTMENT OF NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR) FILED BY PETITIONER MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & COMPANY'S NOTICE OF PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND APPOINTMENT OF NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. PETITIONER SHOULD ADDITIONALLY SEEK A STAY OF ALL PROCEEDINGS PENDING IN RIVERSIDE COUNTY RELATED TO THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THESE PARTIES. THE COURT ADDS TO THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT REASONS THAT THERE IS A CLEAR AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. SAN FRANCISCO IS PROPER VENUE. ALLEGATION OF FRAUD IS ARBITRATABLE. DEFENDANTS ALLEGATIONS GO TO ARBITRATION CLAUSE ITSELF. THE COMPLAINT IS NOT A PROVISIONAL REMEDY. THE COURT DOES NOT APPOINT THE NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration And Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & COMPANY'S Notice of Petition for Order Compelling Arbitration and Appointment of Neutral Arbitrator. Appearances: Darrell Palmer, Esq., of Law Offices of Darrell Palmer, (619) 985-1099, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Respondent, and opposing party; Michael E. Hale, Esq., of Liuzzi, Murphy & Solomon, LLP (415) 543-5050, appearing for Petitioner and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition for order compelling arbitration is granted. Petitioner should additionally seek a stay of all proceedings pending in Riverside County related to the agreement between these parties. The Court adds to the tentative ruling as follows: The Court reasons that there is a clear agreement to arbitrate. San Francisco is Proper Venue. Allegation of Fraud is arbitratable. Defendants allegations go to arbitration clause itself. The complaint is not a provisional remedy. The Court does not appoint the Neutral Arbitrator. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
